```python
intended_family = set(1 << qubit_index for qubit_index in range(QUBIT_COUNT))
counts_list = list(counts.items())
max_count = max(counts_list, key=lambda x: x[1])
assert all(bin(key)[2:].count('1') == 1 for key, _ in counts_list), "Measured counts are not in the intended family."
assert max_count[1] / shots < 0.9, "Probability mass is collapsed onto just one outcome."
```

```python
intended_family = set()
for qubit_index in range(metadata['QUBIT_COUNT']):
    intended_family.add(int(f'1{qubit_index:04b}', 2))

counts_list = list(counts.keys())
assert all(
    count in intended_family 
    for count in counts_list
), f"Measured counts {counts_list} are not in the intended family {intended_family}"

assert len(counts) > 1, f"Probability mass is collapsed onto one outcome: {counts}"
```

```python
intended_basis_states = [1 << i for i in range(QUBIT_COUNT)]
counts_array = [counts.get(state, 0) for state in range(2**QUBIT_COUNT)]
assert all(count == 0 for state, count in enumerate(counts_array) if state not in intended_basis_states), "Counts outside intended basis states"
assert len(counts) > 1, "Probability mass collapsed onto a single outcome"
```

```python
intended_family = set(1 << qubit_index for qubit_index in range(QUBIT_COUNT))
```
```python
counts_array = [counts.get(bin(i)[2:].zfill(QUBIT_COUNT), 0) for i in range(2**QUBIT_COUNT)]
assert sum(counts_array) == shots
assert all(count == 0 for i, count in enumerate(counts_array) if i not in intended_family)
assert max(counts_array) / shots < 0.9  # ensure probability mass is not collapsed onto one outcome
```

```python
intended_basis_states = [1 << i for i in range(QUBIT_COUNT)]
counts_array = [counts.get(bin(state)[2:].zfill(QUBIT_COUNT), 0) for state in range(2**QUBIT_COUNT)]
assert sum(counts_array[i] for i in intended_basis_states) / shots > 0.9, "Probability mass escaped the intended family of basis states"
assert max(counts_array) / shots < 0.7, "Probability mass is collapsed onto a single basis state"
```

```python
intended_family = set(1 << qubit_index for qubit_index in range(metadata['QUBIT_COUNT']))
```

```python
for outcome, _ in counts_list:

assert len(counts) > 1, "Probability mass is collapsed onto a single outcome"
```

```python
intended_basis_states = [1 << i for i in range(QUBIT_COUNT)]
counts_list = list(counts.items())
assert all(
    basis_state in intended_basis_states 
    for basis_state, _ in counts_list

assert len(counts_list) > 1, "Probability mass is collapsed onto a single outcome."
```

```python
intended_basis_states = [1 << i for i in range(QUBIT_COUNT)]
counts_array = [counts.get(state, 0) for state in range(2**QUBIT_COUNT)]
assert sum(counts_array) == shots, "Counts do not sum up to the total shots"
assert all(count >= 0 for count in counts_array), "Negative counts are present"
probability_mass = [count / shots for count in counts_array]
assert sum(probability_mass) == 1.0, "Probabilities do not sum up to 1.0"
assert max(probability_mass) <= 1 / QUBIT_COUNT + 1e-6, "Probability mass is collapsed onto one state"
assert all(state in intended_basis_states or count == 0 for state, count in counts.items()), "Counts are present for states outside the intended basis states"
```

```python
intended_basis_states = [1 << i for i in range(QUBIT_COUNT)]
counts_list = list(counts.items())

# Check that all present outcomes correspond to the intended basis states
for outcome, _ in counts_list:
    outcome_int = int(outcome, 2)
    assert outcome_int in intended_basis_states, f"Unexpected outcome {outcome}"

# Check that the probability mass is not collapsed onto just one outcome
assert len(counts_list) > 1, "Probability mass collapsed onto a single outcome"
```

```python
intended_basis_states = [bin(i)[2:].zfill(metadata['QUBIT_COUNT']) for i in range(metadata['QUBIT_COUNT'])]
counts_list = list(counts.items())
valid_outcomes = any(outcome[0] in intended_basis_states for outcome in counts_list)
assert valid_outcomes, f"Measured counts {counts} are not in the intended basis states {intended_basis_states}"
assert len(counts) > 1, f"Measured counts {counts} are collapsed onto a single outcome"
```

```python
intended_basis_states = [1 << i for i in range(QUBIT_COUNT)]
counts_list = list(counts.items())
valid_outcomes = [outcome for outcome, _ in counts_list if int(outcome, 2) in intended_basis_states]

assert len(valid_outcomes) > 0, "No valid outcomes found in counts"
assert len(counts_list) > 1, "Counts are collapsed onto a single outcome"
assert sum(counts[outcome] for outcome in valid_outcomes) / shots > 0.9, "Probability mass is not concentrated in the intended family of basis states"
```
